reasons: Integration diagnostics explicitly flag mixed list encodings in fused output for industry and _fusion_sources (json_list_string vs malformed_json_like), which can break exact comparisons and introduce noise.

Normalization sensitivity report shows industry has 2 non-missing kinds (8398 malformed_json_like vs 3109 json_list_string) and _fusion_sources has 2 kinds (12437 malformed_json_like vs 190 json_list_string), indicating canonicalization is not stable.
Validation hints indicate country output format should be name; keeping country canonicalization aligned to this is appropriate (country accuracy is high at 0.96 and no sample mismatches were shown).
No evidence of case-only mismatches (0), so broad lowercasing of value columns is not justified; only provenance/id-style columns should be lowercased per validation hints.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun normalization focusing on repairing/standardizing list-like encodings for industry and _fusion_sources to a consistent JSON array string representation, and keep country canonicalized to validation-style country names. Do not apply blanket lowercasing beyond the validation-indicated provenance/id columns since there are zero case-only mismatches.

findings.title: Attribute-level source agreement shows dominant sources

details.interpretation: For some attributes, one source matches fused values far more often than others; consider trust-based source preference per attribute.

findings.title: Low attribute accuracies in evaluation indicate fusion/matching gaps
One-to-many correspondences suggest over-matching / ambiguous links

details.interpretation: Many left-entities link to multiple right-entities; consider increasing match threshold or improving blocking features.

recommendations.evidence_summary: Correspondence file 'correspondences_dbpedia_metacritic.csv' shows high one-to-many ratio (0.537) over 36142 rows, suggesting over-matching.

suggested_changes.notes: Use more discriminative keys (e.g., normalized name + country/city).

PyDI.fusion.evaluation module

Evaluation framework for data fusion in PyDI.
This module provides tools for evaluating the quality of fusion results
against validation/test datasets.
